title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government's response outlines steps to implement recommendations from a 2020 report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards. This includes measures to address concerns and ensure that AI development aligns with UK standards and values.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document explores the relationship between copyright law and the development of artificial intelligence. It assesses the potential impact of copyright regulations on AI systems that use copyrighted works for development.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education's policy on generative AI in education is aimed at guiding the use of this technology in educational settings to ensure its benefits are maximized while potential risks are minimized. The policy likely provides guidelines and regulations on how generative AI should be implemented in schools and educational institutions.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan is a document that outlines how the Department for Transport (DfT) will work with the transport sector to use artificial intelligence effectively. It aims to balance the benefits of AI with potential risks in the transport industry.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This progress report focuses on the use of copyrighted works in developing Artificial Intelligence systems. It is based on a report under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations from the Regulatory Horizons Council on how to regulate artificial intelligence used in medical devices. This response will outline the government's actions to ensure the safe and effective use of AI in the medical industry.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual is creating a framework to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications. This is in response to a request from key government figures to ensure AI's impact on the qualifications sector is managed effectively.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government response outlines its plans and priorities in reaction to the House of Lords Select Committee's recommendations for artificial intelligence. The response aims to address concerns and issues raised by the committee in their report 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee reviewing the relationship between artificial intelligence and public standards. The goal is to provide guidelines for the committee's investigation into how AI intersects with public standards.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has submitted its views to a parliamentary inquiry into the governance of Artificial Intelligence. The submission aims to contribute to a better understanding of the governance of AI.
